source: clang-tidy, cppcheck, CERT C Coding Standard

rules.name: malloc-without-free
rules.message: malloc() call — ensure corresponding free() exists to prevent memory leaks.
rules.fix_hint: Verify every malloc has a matching free on all code paths, including error paths.

rules.name: buffer-overflow-risk
rules.message: Unsafe function with buffer overflow risk — use bounds-checked alternative.
rules.fix_hint: Replace gets→fgets, sprintf→snprintf, strcpy→strncpy or strlcpy, strcat→strncat or strlcat.

rules.name: hardcoded-credentials
rules.fix_hint: Move secrets to environment variables or a secure configuration file; load via getenv().

rules.name: console-output
rules.message: printf() in production code — use a logging framework instead.
rules.fix_hint: Replace with syslog(), a project-specific logger, or guard with a debug/verbose flag.

rules.name: deep-nesting
rules.message: Deep nesting detected (4+ indent levels) — reduces readability.
rules.fix_hint: Extract inner logic into named functions or use early returns / goto cleanup pattern.

rules.name: magic-numbers
rules.message: Magic number in comparison — extract to a named constant or #define for clarity.
rules.fix_hint: Define a #define, enum value, or static const with a descriptive name.

rules.name: format-string-vulnerability
rules.message: printf() with variable format string — potential format string vulnerability, use printf("%s", var) instead.
rules.fix_hint: Use a literal format string or validate user-provided format strings.

rules.name: malloc-without-nearby-free
rules.message: malloc() without nearby free() — verify memory is properly freed to prevent leaks.
rules.fix_hint: Verify every malloc has a matching free on all code paths.

rules.name: unchecked-return-value
rules.message: Return value of error-reporting function not checked — may silently fail.
rules.fix_hint: Check the return value (NULL for allocation/fopen, count for fread/fwrite) and handle the error.

rules.name: null-pointer-dereference-risk
rules.message: Pointer dereference via -> — ensure pointer is validated as non-NULL before use.
rules.fix_hint: Add a NULL check before dereferencing, or use assert() for invariant enforcement.

rules.name: api-key-exposure
rules.message: Possible API key or access token in source code
rules.fix_hint: Move to environment variables or a secrets vault. Never commit secrets.

rules.name: private-key-block
rules.message: Private key detected in source code
rules.fix_hint: Remove private key from source. Use key management service or environment variable.

rules.name: connection-string-credentials
rules.message: Connection string with embedded credentials detected
rules.fix_hint: Use environment variables for connection strings. Never embed passwords in URIs.

rules.name: email-in-source
rules.message: Email address detected in source — verify this is not PII
rules.fix_hint: If this is a real email address, consider moving to configuration. Test fixtures should use @example.com.

file_size._note: Lines of code. C files tend to be longer due to explicit memory management and boilerplate.

function_size._note: Detected via brace-counting heuristic, not grep.
